title: Eva 30 Film Reviews
wikitext:
[]: Eva 3.0 Film Reviews             Quote Post by JeffersonLeeEng » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:21 pm Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Comprehend New Eva film blows minds across Japan by Ryotaro Aoki There was a moment of awkward silence once the house lights came up in the theater after the end credits of Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo. To Evangelion veterans who were there the first time around, it was probably a familiar feeling. To everyone else, it was probably more like a big collective “huh?”

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Nov 20, 2012 6:12 pm Didn't read beyond the spoiler warning in the article. No spoiler biggie. But, GODDAMN, ANNO! I'm feeling the exact same kinds of dreadful anticipation as I was watching NGE for the first time. I forgot how horrible that felt...

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Nov 20, 2012 6:44 pm I know what you mean, and for the first time since I discovered Eva about 2 years ago, I have that sensation like Neo learning Kung-fu in the first matrix film having absorbed a buttload of information in a short space of time.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:24 pm This must be how the Japanese fan's felt in the 90's

[]:             Quote Post by Xavierla » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:18 pm how are people seeing this movie. I've been told numerous times that Eva 3.0 is out yet in foreign countries.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:22 pm A number of people have taken trips to Japan for purposes of seeing the movie; others are already in the country for other reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed Dec 05, 2012 7:24 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: This must be how the Japanese fan's felt in the 90's

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Dec 05, 2012 8:31 pm Blue Monday wrote: I commend the people who are keeping themselves spoiler-free. Good on you! I understand it's going to be a hard wait, but it's going to be so worth it when you finally see it!

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Dec 05, 2012 8:40 pm Mr. Tines wrote: A number of people have taken trips to Japan for purposes of seeing the movie; others are already in the country for other reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Dec 06, 2012 6:44 am Rei IV wrote: Jeez, add the price of an airline ticket to Japan to the cost of a movie ticket in that part of the world, and possibly other "additional expenses" (yes, this includes goodies they usually sell in Japanese theaters), and that's hell of a lot of money for one film.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Dec 06, 2012 7:19 am Come Final I bet a whole bunch of EGFers will be in Tokyo/Japan ready to hit up the midnight screenings, haha. Won't be until about 2015 though, I reckon.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Thu Dec 06, 2012 2:15 pm Blue Monday wrote: Come Final I bet a whole bunch of EGFers will be in Tokyo/Japan ready to hit up the midnight screenings, haha. Won't be until about 2015 though, I reckon.

[]:             Quote Post by Xavierla » Sun Dec 09, 2012 4:58 pm Where did you watch it from anyway it isn't out in the U.S. yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 09, 2012 5:17 pm Xavierla wrote: Where did you watch it from anyway it isn't out in the U.S. yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 03, 2014 6:24 pm Since we only have the Japanese reviews thread , I thought it'd be nice to post this one (January 12th 2013): http://www.isugoi.com/evangelion-3-0-you-can-not-redo-review/

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Jan 03, 2014 6:42 pm ^I should probably avoid this.....I fear that I'll become really butthurt after reading the review...... This is the only really positive Eva 3.0 review I've found so far: [url]http://www.graffitiwithpunctuation.net/2013/09/25/reel-anime-2013-review-centerpiece-evangelion-3-0-can-redo/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 03, 2014 6:46 pm IMO it was a positive review, since a lot of the ones I've read seemed to be either negative or unsure what to think. Oh yeah, I removed the "No Spoilers" from the topic since that's not relevant anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Jan 03, 2014 6:54 pm SSD wrote: isugoi.com

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 03, 2014 7:08 pm Weirdly enough (at least from what I've heard), he left the fandom because of 3.0. I do understand why fans are split due to 3.0 (Maya's seiyuu predicted it a few years back), but I don't find reactions terribly different to the TV ending or EoE. Or Death & Rebirth not being the "proper" ending to the series (that's why the hatemail was written). 3.0 really feels like a prelude to Final if anything else, so if Final fails to answer the big stuff that needs answering, then fans can complain all they like. That's my take, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jan 03, 2014 8:04 pm Fans are fickle. Anime fans - who might very be the most picky, spoiled, shallow minded, entitled fandom of all - are especially fickle. No surprise 3.0 upset a few. I'm quite positive 4.0 will leave a large amount of people upset no matter what the film does. As long as the film doesn't upset me is all that matters.... to me.
summary: []: Eva 3.0 Film Reviews             Quote Post by JeffersonLeeEng » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:21 pm Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Comprehend New Eva film blows minds across Japan by Ryotaro Aoki There was a moment of awkward silence once the house lights came up in the theater after the end credits of Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo. To Evangelion veterans who were there the first time around, it was probably a familiar feeling
